skill.name: sitemapkit
skill.description: Discover and extract sitemaps from any website using SitemapKit. Use this skill whenever the user wants to find pages on a website, get a list of URLs from a domain, audit a site's structure, crawl a sitemap, check what pages exist on a site, or do anything involving sitemaps or site URL discovery — even if they don't explicitly say "sitemap". Requires the sitemapkit MCP server configured with a valid SITEMAPKIT_API_KEY.
skill.summary: Discover and extract URLs from website sitemaps for SEO auditing and site structure analysis
skill.author: 0nl1n1n

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate MCP server for SitemapKit that discovers and extracts sitemaps from websites. After evaluating 334 static findings, all high-severity flags are false positives. The skill uses standard API calls to sitemapkit.com service with proper API key handling via environment variables. No malicious code execution or data exfiltration patterns were found.

medium_findings.title: External API Dependency
medium_findings.description: Skill relies on external sitemapkit.com API service. Users should verify service availability and terms of service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: API_BASE URL is hardcoded to sitemapkit.com - legitimate third-party service dependency

low_findings.title: Environment Variable API Key
low_findings.description: API key stored in SITEMAPKIT_API_KEY environment variable - standard secure practice
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: API key access is properly handled via environment variable, not hardcoded

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick syntax in markdown files as shell execution. These are documentation code blocks, not actual code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown files contain usage examples with backtick formatting. Source code uses fetch() API, not shell commands.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Cryptographic Warnings
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged weak crypto (md5/sha1) and high-entropy strings in package-lock.json. These are standard npm package hashes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: package-lock.json contains standard npm registry URLs and dependency hashes that appear random

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - C2 Keywords
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged C2 keywords in package-lock.json. These are legitimate npm package names that coincidentally match keyword patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: NPM package names in lock file matching security scanner patterns - legitimate dependencies

content.user_title: Discover and extract website sitemaps with SitemapKit
content.value_statement: Finding and extracting sitemap data from websites requires manual crawling and parsing. SitemapKit automates sitemap discovery and URL extraction through a simple API, enabling Claude to audit site structures and gather URL lists efficiently.
actual_capabilities: Discover all sitemap files for any domain by checking robots.txt, common paths, and sitemap indexes
Extract complete URL lists from sitemap files including metadata like lastmod dates
Full crawl mode that discovers sitemaps and extracts all URLs in one operation
Quota tracking to monitor API usage and plan limits
Automatic handling of sitemap indexes that link to child sitemaps
limitations: Requires valid SITEMAPKIT_API_KEY and internet connection
API rate limits and monthly quota apply based on subscription plan
Cannot discover sitemaps on sites that block sitemapkit.com service
Results may be truncated at plan limits for large websites

use_cases.title: SEO Auditors
use_cases.description: Quickly audit client websites by discovering their sitemap structure and extracting URL lists for comprehensive SEO analysis.

use_cases.title: Web Developers
use_cases.description: Get complete page lists from competitor or reference sites for competitive analysis and structure comparison.
use_cases.target_user: Web developers and technical SEO specialists

use_cases.title: Content Researchers
use_cases.description: Discover all published content URLs from a domain for content auditing, broken link checking, or content inventory.

prompt_templates.title: Basic sitemap discovery
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to know what sitemap files exist on a domain

prompt_templates.title: Full site URL extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Get all pages from https://shop.example.com including their last modified dates
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants complete URL inventory with metadata

prompt_templates.title: Specific sitemap extraction
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a specific sitemap URL they want to parse

prompt_templates.title: Site structure audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the sitemap structure of https://docs.example.com - tell me how many sitemaps exist and how many total URLs are indexed
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a complete site structure overview

best_practices: Use discover_sitemaps first to understand the site structure before extracting specific sitemaps
Check quota status in responses to plan ahead for large crawling operations
When results are truncated, increase max_urls parameter or suggest upgrading the plan to users
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to crawl websites that explicitly block automated access in their robots.txt
Avoid making rapid successive requests - respect rate limits shown in API responses
Do not share extracted data in ways that violate the target website's terms of service

faq.question: What is SitemapKit?
faq.answer: SitemapKit is a web service that discovers and extracts sitemap data from websites. It provides a simple API to find sitemap files and extract their URL contents.

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Sign up at sitemapkit.com and get your API key from the settings page at app.sitemapkit.com/settings/api

faq.question: What happens if I exceed my quota?
faq.answer: The API returns a quota exceeded error. You can upgrade your plan or wait until the next billing cycle for quota reset.

faq.question: Can I extract all URLs from a large website?
faq.answer: Yes, but large sites may exceed plan limits. The response shows a truncated flag when limits are hit, and you can increase max_urls up to your plan maximum.

faq.question: Does SitemapKit work with all websites?
faq.answer: SitemapKit works with sites that publish accessible sitemaps. Sites without sitemaps or that block the service cannot be crawled.

faq.question: What metadata is included in extracted URLs?
faq.answer: URLs include optional lastmod (last modified date), changefreq (change frequency), and priority fields when present in the sitemap.
